NovaStackv0.1.0
GitHub
NovaStack CLI MVP is officially live

Build production-ready applications before your coffee gets cold.

Opinionated open-source CLI that scaffolds modern full-stack Next.js applications with optimal configuration and production defaults.

View GitHub
$npx novastack create
bash — NovaStack CLI
$
Core Engine Foundations
Next.jsTypeScriptPrismaPostgreSQLTailwind CSSBetter AuthDocker
Comparison

Why NovaStack?

Manual Integration

Initial Setup Time

Typically 1 to 2 hours of copy-pasting configs

Decision Fatigue

Evaluating multiple ORMs, auth providers, and template frameworks

Version Compatibility

Debugging peer dependency conflicts between Next.js, Prisma, and React 19

Authentication Integration

Setting up custom tables, JWT sessions, and client hooks manually

Container Configuration

Writing optimal multi-stage Dockerfiles and compose setups manually

Folder Architecture

Ad-hoc structuring of app/ routes, components, and library client files

Result: High configuration latency, context switching, brittle setup.

NovaStack Golden path

Initial Setup Time

30 seconds with a single command

Decision Fatigue

Pre-selected golden stack of industry-standard tools

Version Compatibility

Guaranteed lockfile compilation with verified, tested packages

Authentication Integration

Ready-to-use Better Auth configuration with login, schema, and API routes

Container Configuration

Production-ready Docker build stages pre-configured from start

Folder Architecture

Consistent layout optimized for performance and team scaling

Result: Zero latency, ready for production instantly, clean builds.
The Stack Blueprint

The Golden Stack

Blueprint Spec

Next.js 15

Layer Type: Framework Layer

Configured with App Router, server actions, optimized layouts, and standard error boundaries. Zero runtime configuration needed.

Audited & verified dependency configuration
Target: node >= 18.0.0
Core Features

Engineered for DX

current

Project Scaffolding

Initialize full-stack structure with standard Next.js layouts, App Router endpoints, client utils, and models instantly.

current

Production Defaults

Comes with standard setups for error handling, global loading views, environment variable templates, and strict TypeScript configurations.

current

Docker Support

Includes a production-ready multi-stage Dockerfile and an optimized compose file for development Postgres instances.

current

Better Auth Integration

Includes predefined Prisma schemas and Next.js handlers for email and password authentication out of the box.

current

Prisma Integration

Database connection pools, seeding setups, schema structures, and client singletons prepared for high scale.

current

Strict TypeScript

Types defined for database clients, scaffolding pipelines, env validations, and authentication helpers.

upcoming

Doctor Diagnosis CLI

Analyze environment variable setup, Docker container status, schema validity, and linting standards directly in the terminal.

upcoming

Module Generators

Execute short commands to scaffold database tables, API routes, layout pages, and UI modules inline with project conventions.

upcoming

NovaStack Plugin System

Build, distribute, and install custom CLI rules and template overrides to share project standards across teams.

CLI reference

Command Directory

novastack create

Command

Initialize and scaffold a new full-stack Next.js project with the pre-configured golden stack.

Usage
npx novastack create [project-name] [options]
Options / Flags
[name]Name of the project directory (optional)
--no-installSkip dependency installation
--no-gitSkip initializing git repository
Output
Creating project: my-app
✔ Rendering templates
✔ Writing files
✔ Installing dependencies
✔ Initializing git
Project is ready! Run "cd my-app && npm run dev" to start.
System Assembly

Internal Pipeline Architecture

Step 01

CLI Interface

Captures user input and overrides flags.

Step 02

Blueprint Engine

Resolves dependency lockfiles and core targets.

Step 03

Modules

Configures settings for Auth, DB, Docker, and UI.

Step 04

Template Renderer

Compiles TypeScript template source files.

Step 05

Project Gen

Writes project workspace files to local disk.

Step 06

Production App

A fully runnable, production-ready system.

The NovaStack compiler pipelines user requirements through a structured assembly layer, compiling dynamic templates directly to local system directories with zero external runtime overhead.
Compiler pipeline version 1.0.0
Timeline

Development Milestones

v0.1

Create Command Scaffolding

v0.1

Create Command Scaffolding

Scaffolding of the Next.js core, Prisma schema definitions, Better Auth adapters, and Tailwind v4 setup.

complete
v0.2

Doctor System Diagnostics

v0.2

Doctor System Diagnostics

Configuration checks, connection pooling testing, environment variable validation, and local container auditing.

in progress
v0.3

Module Generator Tools

v0.3

Module Generator Tools

Interactive boilerplate generation of CRUD API endpoints, client components, and database models mapped inline.

planned
v0.4

NovaStack module Registry

v0.4

NovaStack module Registry

Shared community module installation platform to easily add Stripe, Redis, Resend, and standard helper libraries.

planned
v1.0

Cloud Dashboard Platform

v1.0

Cloud Dashboard Platform

Cloud sync configurations, remote templates, team standard share features, and telemetry analytics options.

planned
Frequently Asked Questions

Questions & Answers

Open Source

Built in Public

GitHub Repository

100% Open Source & Community-First

NovaStack is licensed under the permissive MIT License. We believe in transparency, local ownership of code, and community-driven expansion. There are no proprietary lock-ins or telemetry tracking scripts active in core commands.

MIT License

Use it in commercial or personal projects freely.

Contributors Welcome

Submit pull requests, issue fixes, or template proposals.

Join the GitHub Community
